name: Jundab ibn Hijr
title: Veteran Companion
shortDescription: A noble companion of the Prophet (PBUH) and early Shia who was under pressure in Kufa but managed to reach Karbala to help the Master of Martyrs.
fullStory:
Jundab ibn Hijr al-Kindi al-Khawlani was a veteran companion of the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and an early, sincere Shia of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). He participated in the battles during Imam Ali's (AS) era and was later considered a close companion of Imam Hasan (AS). Jundab lived in Kufa and, during the oppressive eras of Muawiyah and Ibn Ziyad, was among the well-known Shia figures constantly under surveillance.

When Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad came to Kufa and made conditions extremely harsh for Shias, Jundab could not stay. Knowing that Imam Hussain (AS) was moving towards Iraq, despite his old age, he secretly left Kufa through off-road paths to reach the Imam's caravan.

Jundab joined the Hussaini caravan before Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi's army blocked the Imam's path. The presence of this elderly and respected companion was a source of encouragement for the Imam's supporters and a testament to the righteousness of the Master of Martyrs' uprising.

On the day of Ashura, despite his old age, Jundab put on battle armor like a brave youth. After getting permission from Imam Hussain (AS), he entered the battlefield, reciting war poetry and defending the oppressed Ahl al-Bayt, and attacked the Kufan army.

After a brave battle and killing a number of enemies, he fell to the ground in the battlefield due to severe injuries and attained the great grace of martyrdom, immortalizing his name among the martyrs of Karbala.
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Jundab ibn Hijr.
